avenues.name: Grid-enhancing tech & reconductoring
avenues.thesis: Advanced conductors + GETs unlock large grid capacity on existing rights-of-way in months, not years — the cheapest, fastest fix for the bottleneck that actually binds; under-deployed for incentive/regulatory reasons, not technical ones.
avenues.signpost: ≥50 GW of incremental US grid capacity unlocked via GETs/advanced transmission tech by end-2028

avenues.name: Solar + storage (utility-scale)
avenues.thesis: Cheapest and quickest-to-deploy new generation; batteries convert it toward firm. The constraint is interconnection, not cost.
avenues.signpost: combined solar+wind share of US generation ≥21% by end-2027 (EIA STEO)

avenues.name: Enhanced geothermal (EGS)
avenues.thesis: Firm, clean, 24/7 power from engineered hot-rock reservoirs using oil-and-gas drilling technique; first commercial-scale plant arriving now with a steep cost-down curve.
avenues.signpost: Fervo Cape Station Phase 1 (100 MW) delivering to grid by end-2026; 500 MW by end-2028

avenues.name: Nuclear restarts & uprates
avenues.thesis: Fastest firm CLEAN megawatts available today — restarting or uprating existing reactors — but capped to a handful of restartable sites.
avenues.signpost: Crane Clean Energy Center (TMI Unit 1, 837 MW) returns to commercial operation by 2028

avenues.name: Onshore wind
avenues.thesis: Still among the cheapest new-build, but siting/permitting headwinds and a sharp LCOE rise are slowing additions.
avenues.signpost: net US onshore wind additions remain positive through 2027

avenues.name: Natural gas CCGT
avenues.thesis: The incumbent firm bridge — indispensable near-term, but not clean, and now equipment-constrained: large-frame turbines are effectively sold out through 2030.
avenues.signpost: new-build CCGT turbine lead times remain ≥4 years through 2028

avenues.name: Nuclear SMRs
avenues.thesis: Potentially transformative firm clean power, but every first unit is a first-of-a-kind; no Western SMR is yet in commercial operation.
avenues.signpost: a US SMR delivers commercial power to the grid by end-2030 (Kairos/Oklo/TerraPower)

avenues.name: Offshore wind
avenues.thesis: Real resource, hard path — most expensive mainstream new-build and highly exposed to policy reversal.
avenues.signpost: Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind (~2.6 GW) reaches full commercial operation on its stated schedule

avenues.name: Fusion
avenues.thesis: Physics of net energy is settled; the open questions are engineering, cost, and timeline. A 2027 proof point is worth watching; grid-relevant scale is 2030s-plus.
avenues.signpost: CFS SPARC demonstrates net energy gain (Q>1) by end-2027

avenues.name: Space-based solar power
avenues.thesis: Proven in principle (orbit-to-ground beaming demonstrated), but full-system economics remain decades out — a long-horizon hedge, not a near-term source.
avenues.signpost: a paying, revenue space-to-grid power delivery occurs by 2030

checks._comment: Single source of truth for the consistency-check RULES. Both index.html's console and verification/verify_numbers.py read these, so a threshold can never drift between the page and the verifier. Change a rule HERE and both sides enforce it on next run. (The check LOGIC is still written in each language — see AUTHORING.md — but the rules it enforces live only here.)
